title: A New Friend
passage:
Making new friends can feel hard, __(1)__ it does not have to be. When I moved to a new town last year, I knew nobody at my new school. On the first day, I felt very __(2)__ and sat alone at lunch. Then a girl named Mei came over and asked __(3)__ she could sit with me. We started talking and soon found that we both loved drawing.
Mei introduced me to her friends, and they were all very __(4)__ to me. They invited me to join their art club, and I happily agreed. __(5)__ , I no longer felt like a stranger. I learned an important lesson that year: if you want to make friends, you should be brave and open your heart. A small smile or a kind word can __(6)__ change someone's whole day.

blanks.stem: Making new friends can feel hard, __(1)__ it does not have to be.
blanks.explanation: 前後語意轉折（交朋友看似困難，但不必如此），用對等連接詞 but。

blanks.stem: On the first day, I felt very __(2)__ and sat alone at lunch.
blanks.explanation: 剛轉學、午餐獨自一人坐，最符合的心情是孤單 lonely。

blanks.stem: Then a girl named Mei came over and asked __(3)__ she could sit with me.
blanks.explanation: asked 後接間接問句，表示「是否」可以坐下，用 if。

blanks.stem: They were all very __(4)__ to me.
blanks.explanation: 他們邀我加入社團，可見對我很友善 friendly；rude 無禮語意相反。

blanks.stem: __(5)__ , I no longer felt like a stranger.
blanks.explanation: 加入社團後「很快地」就不再覺得自己是陌生人，用 Soon 表時間推進最連貫。

blanks.stem: A small smile or a kind word can __(6)__ change someone's whole day.
blanks.explanation: 強調一個微笑或善意「真的」能改變某人的一天，用 really；其餘三個都是否定語意，與正面結論矛盾。

title: Get Moving!
passage:
Many doctors say that exercise is one of the best __(1)__ to stay healthy. Yet a lot of students spend most of their free time sitting in front of screens. They often feel tired __(2)__ they do not move enough during the day. The good news is that getting active is __(3)__ than most people think.
You do not need expensive equipment or a gym. __(4)__ , you can simply take a walk after dinner or ride a bike to school. Doing sports with friends is even better, because it is more fun and you can encourage each other. Experts suggest that young people should exercise __(5)__ thirty minutes a day. If you build this habit early, your body will stay strong and your mind will feel __(6)__ .

blanks.stem: Many doctors say that exercise is one of the best __(1)__ to stay healthy.
blanks.explanation: one of the best ways to stay healthy 指保持健康的最佳方法之一，用 ways。

blanks.stem: They often feel tired __(2)__ they do not move enough during the day.
blanks.explanation: 感到疲倦的原因是白天活動量不足，用表原因的連接詞 because。

blanks.stem: Getting active is __(3)__ than most people think.
blanks.explanation: than 表比較，下文說明不需器材或健身房，可知運動比想像中「更容易」，用比較級 easier。

blanks.stem: __(4)__ , you can simply take a walk after dinner or ride a bike to school.
blanks.explanation: 前句說不需健身房，後句提出替代做法（散步、騎車），用表「取而代之」的 Instead。

blanks.stem: Experts suggest that young people should exercise __(5)__ thirty minutes a day.
blanks.explanation: 專家建議每天運動「至少」三十分鐘，用 at least；at last 是終於、at most 是至多，語意不合。

blanks.stem: Your body will stay strong and your mind will feel __(6)__ .
blanks.explanation: 養成運動習慣後身體強壯、心靈也感到清爽舒暢，用 fresh；其餘都是負面語意，與正面結論矛盾。

title: Staying Safe Online
passage:
The internet is a wonderful tool, __(1)__ it can also be dangerous if we are not careful. Many young people share photos and personal information online __(2)__ thinking about who might see them. Once something is posted, it can be very __(3)__ to remove it completely.
To stay safe, you should never give your password to anyone, __(4)__ your close friends. It is also smart to check the privacy settings on your apps. __(5)__ , if a stranger sends you a strange link, do not click on it. Being careful online does not mean you cannot have fun. __(6)__ , it means you can enjoy the internet without worrying about your safety.

blanks.stem: The internet is a wonderful tool, __(1)__ it can also be dangerous if we are not careful.
blanks.explanation: 前後語意轉折（網路很棒，但不小心也危險），用對等連接詞 but。

blanks.stem: Many young people share photos and personal information online __(2)__ thinking about who might see them.
blanks.explanation: 「沒有先想清楚誰會看到」就分享，用 without thinking。

blanks.stem: Once something is posted, it can be very __(3)__ to remove it completely.
blanks.explanation: 東西一旦張貼出去，要「完全」刪除會很困難，用 hard。

blanks.stem: You should never give your password to anyone, __(4)__ your close friends.
blanks.explanation: 不要把密碼給任何人，「包括」最好的朋友也一樣，用 including 強調連他們也不行。

blanks.stem: __(5)__ , if a stranger sends you a strange link, do not click on it.
blanks.explanation: 再補充一個安全提醒，用表「此外」的 Also。

blanks.stem: __(6)__ , it means you can enjoy the internet without worrying about your safety.
blanks.explanation: 小心上網不代表不能玩，「反而」能安心享受網路，用 Instead。

title: Life with a Pet
passage:
Many children dream of having a pet, __(1)__ taking care of one is a big responsibility. A pet is not a toy; it is a living thing that needs love and attention every day. Before you get a pet, you should think __(2)__ about whether your family has enough time for it.
Dogs, for example, need to be walked __(3)__ , sometimes twice a day. They also need fresh water and healthy food. If you are often busy, a fish or a turtle might be a __(4)__ choice. __(5)__ the kind of pet you choose, you must clean its home and take it to the animal doctor when it is sick. Caring for a pet teaches children to be kind and __(6)__ .

blanks.stem: Many children dream of having a pet, __(1)__ taking care of one is a big responsibility.
blanks.explanation: 前後語意轉折（很多孩子想養寵物，但照顧是很大的責任），用 but。

blanks.stem: Before you get a pet, you should think __(2)__ about whether your family has enough time for it.
blanks.explanation: 養寵物前應該「仔細地」想清楚時間夠不夠，用副詞 carefully。

blanks.stem: Dogs, for example, need to be walked __(3)__ , sometimes twice a day.
blanks.explanation: 狗需要「規律地」散步，有時一天兩次，用 regularly；never、seldom 語意相反。

blanks.stem: If you are often busy, a fish or a turtle might be a __(4)__ choice.
blanks.explanation: 若常常很忙，魚或烏龜會是「比較好」（較好照顧）的選擇，用 better。

blanks.stem: __(5)__ the kind of pet you choose, you must clean its home and take it to the animal doctor when it is sick.
blanks.explanation: 「無論」你選哪種寵物，都得清理與就醫，用 No matter 表「不管…」。

blanks.stem: Caring for a pet teaches children to be kind and __(6)__ .
blanks.explanation: 照顧寵物教孩子變得善良又「負責任」，用 responsible；其餘為負面特質。

title: A Summer to Remember
passage:
Last summer, I went on a camping trip with my school. It was my first time sleeping in a tent, so I felt both excited and a little __(1)__. When the bus arrived at the forest campsite, the cool air hit my face, and I knew it would be a great trip.
The first activity was hiking. The trail went up a steep hill, and my legs hurt after just twenty minutes. __(2)__ , the view from the top was worth every step. We could see a sparkling lake below us, and some students started taking photos right away.
For dinner, our teacher taught us __(3)__ to cook rice in a pot over an open fire. I burned mine a little at first, but my second try turned out perfectly. I felt proud __(4)__ I ate every grain by myself.
That night, we lay outside and counted stars. In the city, you can __(5)__ see stars because of the bright lights. But out there in the forest, the sky was filled with them. My friend whispered that there might be a million stars above us. I did not know __(6)__ to believe her, but I smiled anyway — it was a perfect night.

blanks.stem: I felt both excited and a little __(1)__.
blanks.explanation: 第一次睡帳篷、對未知有所擔憂，最貼近「緊張」nervous；excited 已出現在前面，不重複，C/D 語意不合。

blanks.stem: __(2)__ , the view from the top was worth every step.
blanks.explanation: 前句說腿很痛，後句說景色值回票價，前後語意轉折，用 However（然而）。

blanks.stem: Our teacher taught us __(3)__ to cook rice in a pot over an open fire.
blanks.explanation: taught us how to do something 固定用法，表示「怎麼做」；what/when/where 在此語意均不合。

blanks.stem: I felt proud __(4)__ I ate every grain by myself.
blanks.explanation: 「當我把飯一粒不剩地吃完時」感到驕傲，用表時間的從屬連接詞 when；unless/although/until 語意均不合。

blanks.stem: In the city, you can __(5)__ see stars because of the bright lights.
blanks.explanation: 城市燈光強烈，「幾乎看不到」星星，用 hardly；always/often/easily 均與燈光遮蔽的語境矛盾。

blanks.stem: I did not know __(6)__ to believe her, but I smiled anyway.
blanks.explanation: did not know whether to believe 表「不確定是否該相信」，用 whether；what/when/where 無法放入此句型。

title: Learning to Bake
passage:
Two years ago, my grandmother taught me __(1)__ to bake bread. It was harder than I thought. On my first try, I mixed all the ingredients too fast __(2)__ forgot to add enough water. The bread came out hard as a rock, and my whole family laughed.
I did not give up, __(3)__ . I went back to my grandmother's house every weekend and tried again. She always watched __(4)__ and told me what I did wrong. After about a month, I finally made a perfect loaf. When I pulled it out of the oven, the whole kitchen smelled __(5)__ and warm.
Now I bake bread every month. Sometimes I bring a loaf to school and share it with my friends. They always say it tastes amazing. I think the secret is not just the recipe — it is the time and love I put into each loaf. __(6)__ , I believe that with enough practice, anyone can learn something new.

blanks.stem: My grandmother taught me __(1)__ to bake bread.
blanks.explanation: taught me how to bake 固定用法，表示「如何」烤麵包；what/when/where 均不能搭配此句型表達「學做某事」。

blanks.stem: I mixed all the ingredients too fast __(2)__ forgot to add enough water.
blanks.explanation: 攪拌太快「而且」忘了加足夠的水，兩件事並列（都是失誤），用 and；but/or/so 語意不合。

blanks.stem: I did not give up, __(3)__ .
blanks.explanation: 雖然失敗，「然而」我沒有放棄，用 however 表轉折（放在句尾作插入語）；therefore/instead/for example 與此語境不符。

blanks.stem: She always watched __(4)__ and told me what I did wrong.
blanks.explanation: 奶奶「仔細地」觀察並指出錯誤，用副詞 carefully；quickly 與「告訴錯在哪」需要細心矛盾，rarely 否定頻率、loudly 描述聲音均不合。

blanks.stem: When I pulled it out of the oven, the whole kitchen smelled __(5)__ and warm.
blanks.explanation: 新鮮出爐的麵包讓廚房充滿「甜香」warm and sweet；sour/bitter 為負面氣味、cold 與 warm 矛盾。

blanks.stem: __(6)__ , I believe that with enough practice, anyone can learn something new.
blanks.explanation: 前文描述練習讓我成功的過程，「因此」得出結論「只要練習任何人都能學會新事物」，用 Therefore；however 表轉折、instead 表替代、for example 表舉例，皆不合此總結語氣。

title: My First Day at Work
passage:
Last month, I got my first job at a small bakery near my house. I was nervous, __(1)__ I really wanted to learn something new. On my first morning, the owner, Mrs. Lin, showed me how to arrange the bread on the shelves. She spoke slowly and smiled a lot, so I did not feel scared for long. She also introduced me to two other new employees, and we quickly began chatting while arranging the shelves together.
By noon, I __(2)__ how to use the cash register by myself. A few customers asked me questions about the prices, and I answered them without any help. I felt proud of myself.
In the afternoon, a boy __(3)__ looked about my age came in to buy a cake for his mother's birthday. He could not decide which one to choose, so I helped him pick a strawberry cake. He thanked me and left with a big smile. Mrs. Lin praised me for handling the situation so kindly, which made me feel like part of the team already.
When I got home that night, I told my parents about my day. They were happy to hear that my first day went so well. I learned that trying something new is not as scary as it seems, and that a little courage can lead to a wonderful experience.

blanks.stem: I was nervous, __(1)__ I really wanted to learn something new.
blanks.explanation: 雖然緊張，但仍然很想學新事物，前後語意轉折，用對等連接詞 but。

blanks.stem: By noon, I __(2)__ how to use the cash register by myself.
blanks.explanation: 強調在中午這個時間點之前已經完成的動作，須用過去完成式 had learned。

blanks.stem: In the afternoon, a boy __(3)__ looked about my age came in to buy a cake for his mother's birthday.
blanks.explanation: 關係代名詞指人（a boy）且在子句中當主詞（looked 的主詞），用 who。

title: A Rainy Weekend
passage:
Last Saturday, it rained all day, __(1)__ my brother and I stayed home instead of going to the park. At first, we felt a little bored because we could not ride our bikes outside.
Then Mom __(2)__ us to build a blanket fort in the living room. We put pillows everywhere and used a flashlight to make the fort feel like a cave. It was actually a lot of fun. We even invited our neighbor's children to join us, and soon the fort was filled with laughter and silly stories.
In the evening, Dad, __(3)__ loves cooking, made hot chocolate for all of us, and we watched a movie together under the fort. The rain kept falling outside, but we felt warm and happy inside. My little sister fell asleep inside the fort before the movie even ended, still holding her mug of hot chocolate.
By the time we went to bed, I realized that a rainy day at home with my family could be just as fun as a sunny day at the park. Sometimes the best memories come from simple moments we do not plan. Now, whenever it rains on a weekend, my family looks forward to building another fort instead of feeling disappointed about staying inside.

blanks.stem: It rained all day, __(1)__ my brother and I stayed home instead of going to the park.
blanks.explanation: 因為下雨（原因），所以留在家裡（結果），用表結果的連接詞 so。

blanks.stem: Then Mom __(2)__ us to build a blanket fort in the living room.
blanks.explanation: 全文為過去式敘述，此處描述過去發生的動作，須用過去式 suggested。

blanks.stem: In the evening, Dad, __(3)__ loves cooking, made hot chocolate for all of us.
blanks.explanation: 非限定關係子句修飾人（Dad）且在子句中當主詞（loves 的主詞），用 who。

title: My Favorite Season
passage:
Autumn is my favorite season of the year. The weather is not too hot __(1)__ too cold, so it feels just right for outdoor activities. The leaves on the trees slowly turn yellow, orange, and red, and I love walking through the park to see the colors change. Sometimes I bring my sketchbook and try to draw the different shades of the leaves before they fall to the ground.
Every October, my family __(2)__ a small trip to the mountains to see the autumn leaves. We bring a picnic basket and sit under the trees for lunch. My little sister always collects the fallen leaves __(3)__ she likes best and makes bookmarks with them at home. She keeps her favorite ones in a small box and shows them proudly to anyone who visits our house.
I also enjoy autumn because school activities like the sports day and the art fair usually happen during this season. The cool breeze makes it comfortable to run and play outside all day. Teachers also seem more relaxed during this season, often taking classes outside to enjoy the pleasant weather.
When winter comes and the leaves are gone, I always look forward to next autumn. It is a season full of color, comfort, and happy memories with my family.

blanks.stem: The weather is not too hot __(1)__ too cold, so it feels just right for outdoor activities.
blanks.explanation: not too hot or too cold 為固定用法，表示兩者皆非，用 or。

blanks.stem: Every October, my family __(2)__ a small trip to the mountains to see the autumn leaves.
blanks.explanation: Every October 表示習慣性、反覆發生的動作，須用現在簡單式 takes。

blanks.stem: My little sister always collects the fallen leaves __(3)__ she likes best and makes bookmarks with them at home.
blanks.explanation: 關係代名詞指物（leaves）且在子句中當受詞（likes 的受詞），用 that。

title: Helping Mom in the Kitchen
passage:
Every Sunday, I help my mom cook lunch in the kitchen. I do not know much about cooking, __(1)__ I am always willing to learn something new from her. Mom usually lets me wash the vegetables and set the table before we start cooking the main dish. I enjoy this part of the routine because it gives me time to talk with her about my day at school.
Last Sunday, we __(2)__ a new recipe for tomato soup from a cookbook. I cut the tomatoes into small pieces while Mom boiled water in a big pot. It took almost an hour to finish cooking, but the kitchen smelled wonderful the whole time. My dad kept walking into the kitchen to ask if the soup was ready, which made all of us laugh.
My little brother, __(3)__ is only five years old, wanted to help too, so Mom gave him a small spoon to stir the soup carefully. He was very proud of himself when the soup was ready.
When we finally sat down to eat, everyone agreed that the soup tasted amazing. Cooking together on Sundays has become one of my favorite family traditions. I hope we can keep this tradition even after I grow up and move away from home.

blanks.stem: I do not know much about cooking, __(1)__ I am always willing to learn something new from her.
blanks.explanation: 雖然不太會煮飯，但仍願意學習，前後語意轉折，用 but。

blanks.stem: Last Sunday, we __(2)__ a new recipe for tomato soup from a cookbook.
blanks.explanation: Last Sunday 為明確過去時間標誌，須用過去簡單式 tried。

blanks.stem: My little brother, __(3)__ is only five years old, wanted to help too.
blanks.explanation: 關係代名詞指人（brother）且在子句中當主詞（is 的主詞），用 who。

title: The Lost Cat
passage:
Yesterday afternoon, our neighbor's cat, Milo, disappeared from the yard. Everyone was worried, __(1)__ we decided to search the whole neighborhood together. My dad, my sister, and I split up to check different streets near our house. Some of our other neighbors joined the search too, and soon almost ten people were walking through the area calling Milo's name.
We __(2)__ posters with Milo's picture on every corner so that people could help us look for him. A kind old man told us that he had seen a cat that looked like Milo near the small park by the river. He even offered to walk with us partway to show us exactly where he had spotted the cat.
We ran to the park and finally found Milo hiding under a bench, __(3)__ seemed scared and hungry. My sister picked him up gently and carried him home in her arms.
Our neighbor was so relieved to see Milo safe again that she thanked us many times. She even baked cookies for our whole family the next day to show her appreciation. That evening, we all agreed that helping each other made the search much easier and faster, and we promised to check on Milo whenever her owner went out of town.

blanks.stem: Everyone was worried, __(1)__ we decided to search the whole neighborhood together.
blanks.explanation: 因為擔心（原因），所以決定一起尋找（結果），用表結果的 so。

blanks.stem: We __(2)__ posters with Milo's picture on every corner.
blanks.explanation: 敘述過去發生的具體動作，須用過去簡單式 hung。

blanks.stem: We finally found Milo hiding under a bench, __(3)__ seemed scared and hungry.
blanks.explanation: 關係代名詞指動物（Milo）且在子句中當主詞（seemed 的主詞），用 which。

title: A Trip to the Night Market
passage:
Every Friday night, my family likes to visit the night market near our house. The street is full of colorful lights, __(1)__ the smell of grilled food fills the air. My favorite thing to do is walk slowly and look at all the different stalls. Some stalls sell clothes and toys, while others offer games where you can win small prizes.
Last week, we __(2)__ a new stall that sold hand-made dumplings. The owner, a friendly old woman, let us watch her make the dumplings by hand. We bought a box and ate them while sitting on a bench. The dumplings were so delicious that we decided to buy a second box to bring home for breakfast.
My little cousin, __(3)__ had never been to a night market before, was excited about everything she saw. She wanted to try every kind of snack and game we passed.
By the time we went home, we were all tired but happy. But everyone agreed that it had been one of the best Friday nights we had had in months. Night markets are a special part of our culture, and I hope my cousin will visit again with us soon. For me, these Friday nights are some of the happiest memories of my whole childhood.

blanks.stem: The street is full of colorful lights, __(1)__ the smell of grilled food fills the air.
blanks.explanation: 兩件同時存在的事實並列描述，用 and。

blanks.stem: Last week, we __(2)__ a new stall that sold hand-made dumplings.
blanks.explanation: Last week 為過去時間標誌，須用過去簡單式 discovered。

blanks.stem: My little cousin, __(3)__ had never been to a night market before, was excited about everything she saw.
blanks.explanation: 關係代名詞指人（cousin）且在子句中當主詞（had never been 的主詞），用 who。

title: Learning to Swim
passage:
I was afraid of water for most of my childhood, __(1)__ I finally decided to take swimming lessons last summer. My coach told me that fear is normal for beginners and that practice would slowly build my confidence. He also promised that he would stay right beside me in the water until I felt completely comfortable.
During the first few lessons, I __(2)__ how to float on my back without panicking. It felt strange at first, but my coach stayed close by and reminded me to breathe slowly and relax my shoulders. Every time I managed to float for a few seconds longer than before, he cheered loudly from the edge of the pool.
By the fourth week, I met another student, __(3)__ was also nervous about deep water, and we started practicing together. Having someone who understood my fear made the lessons much less stressful. We started challenging each other to swim just one more lap, which somehow made the practice feel like a game.
By the end of summer, I could swim across the pool without stopping. Looking back, I am glad I pushed myself to face something that once scared me so much. I still remember the proud feeling of jumping into the deep end for the very first time.

blanks.stem: I was afraid of water for most of my childhood, __(1)__ I finally decided to take swimming lessons last summer.
blanks.explanation: 從小怕水，但最終仍決定學游泳，前後語意轉折，用 but。

blanks.stem: During the first few lessons, I __(2)__ how to float on my back without panicking.
blanks.explanation: 敘述過去發生的具體階段，須用過去簡單式 learned。

blanks.stem: I met another student, __(3)__ was also nervous about deep water.
blanks.explanation: 關係代名詞指人（student）且在子句中當主詞（was 的主詞），用 who。

title: My Grandfather's Garden
passage:
My grandfather has taken care of a small vegetable garden behind his house for over twenty years. He wakes up early every morning, __(1)__ the best time to water the plants is before the sun gets too hot. He says that spending this quiet time with his plants helps him start the day feeling calm and focused.
Last spring, he __(2)__ me how to plant tomato seeds and take care of young seedlings. At first, I accidentally gave the plants too much water, but my grandfather patiently explained what I had done wrong. He showed me how to check the soil with my fingers to know exactly when the plants needed more water.
Now, every weekend, I visit the garden with my grandfather, __(3)__ has taught me almost everything he knows about growing vegetables. We usually pick fresh vegetables together and bring them home for dinner. My grandmother always says that vegetables grown with love taste better than anything bought from a store.
Working in the garden has taught me patience and the joy of watching something grow slowly over time. I hope to have my own garden someday, just like my grandfather. Until then, I am happy to keep learning from him every single weekend.

blanks.stem: He wakes up early every morning, __(1)__ the best time to water the plants is before the sun gets too hot.
blanks.explanation: 早起的原因是因為在太陽變熱前澆水最好，用表原因的 because。

blanks.stem: Last spring, he __(2)__ me how to plant tomato seeds and take care of young seedlings.
blanks.explanation: Last spring 為明確過去時間標誌，須用過去簡單式 taught。

blanks.stem: I visit the garden with my grandfather, __(3)__ has taught me almost everything he knows about growing vegetables.
blanks.explanation: 關係代名詞指人（grandfather）且在子句中當主詞（has taught 的主詞），用 who。

title: The School Talent Show

title: The Tradition of Mooncakes

title: Why Leaves Change Color
passage:
Every autumn, many trees change their leaves from green to bright shades of yellow, orange, and red. This natural show attracts photographers and nature lovers to parks and mountains every single year. This happens __(1)__ the days become shorter and the temperature slowly drops, sending a signal to the trees that winter is coming. During spring and summer, leaves __(2)__ a green substance called chlorophyll, which helps trees absorb sunlight to make food. As autumn arrives, trees produce less chlorophyll, and other colors that were hidden underneath begin to appear. These hidden colors, such as yellow and orange, had actually been present in the leaves all along.
Scientists, __(3)__ research has helped explain this process, say that the exact colors depend on the weather and the type of tree. title: My First Marathon

title: The Power of a Library Card

title: How Bees Help Our World
passage:
Bees are often seen as small, unimportant insects, __(1)__ they actually play one of the most essential roles in keeping our ecosystem alive. As bees travel from flower to flower collecting nectar, they __(2)__ pollen from one plant to another, allowing new fruits and seeds to form. This process, known as pollination, supports nearly a third of all the food crops that humans depend on. Without this natural service, farmers would need to pollinate their crops by hand, a process that would be slow and expensive.
Unfortunately, bee populations around the world, __(3)__ numbers have dropped sharply over the past two decades, are now facing serious threats from pesticides, habitat loss, and climate change. Scientists warn that this decline could have a __(4)__ effect on global food supplies if it continues. Some researchers estimate that entire types of fruit could disappear from grocery store shelves within a few decades.
To protect bees, some farmers have started planting wildflowers near their fields and reducing the use of harmful chemicals. __(5)__ , environmental groups are asking governments to create stronger laws that protect bee habitats before it becomes too late to reverse the damage. Everyday actions, such as planting flowers on a balcony or avoiding certain pesticides, can also make a real difference.

title: The Story of Origami
passage:
Origami, the traditional art of paper folding, began in Japan many centuries ago and has since spread to almost every corner of the world. Today, people of nearly every culture recognize the paper crane as a familiar and beloved symbol. The exact history is unclear, __(1)__ most historians agree that the practice started as a way to fold paper for religious ceremonies. Paper itself was once so expensive that only wealthy families and temples could afford to use it for folding.
Over time, origami __(2)__ from a formal, ceremonial practice into a popular hobby enjoyed by people of all ages. Simple folded shapes, such as the paper crane, became symbols of peace, hope, and good fortune across many cultures. After the Second World War, the paper crane in particular became closely associated with wishes for peace.
Today, origami artists, __(3)__ creations range from simple animals to complex geometric sculptures, continue to push the boundaries of what paper can become. Some artists even use mathematical formulas to design folds that would have seemed impossible decades ago. A few universities now even offer courses that combine origami with engineering and mathematics.
What makes origami especially __(4)__ is that it requires no scissors, glue, or special tools — just a single sheet of paper and patient hands. __(5)__ , this simple art form has inspired advances in fields as different as space engineering and medicine, where folding techniques help design foldable solar panels and medical stents.

title: Overcoming My Fear of Public Speaking

__(5)__ , I finished the presentation without fainting or forgetting my lines completely, and my classmates even clapped when I sat down. That day taught me that courage does not mean feeling no fear at all — it means speaking anyway. Since then, I have volunteered for two more class presentations, and each one has felt a little less frightening.

title: The Mystery of Migrating Birds
passage:
Every year, millions of birds travel thousands of kilometers between their breeding grounds and their winter homes, __(1)__ scientists still do not fully understand how these small creatures manage such an incredible journey without becoming lost. Some species fly non-stop for several days, crossing oceans and deserts along the way. Some Arctic terns, for example, travel from one pole to the other and back again within a single year.
Researchers have discovered that many birds __(2)__ the Earth's magnetic field as an internal compass, sensing tiny changes that help them stay on course. Others appear to rely on the position of the sun and stars, especially when flying at night. A few species are even believed to recognize familiar landmarks, such as coastlines and mountain ranges, from memory alone.
A team of scientists, __(3)__ decade-long study followed a single flock of Arctic terns, found that these birds may travel a distance equal to flying to the moon and back over their lifetime. Such findings suggest that migration is an extraordinarily __(4)__ skill developed over millions of years of evolution. Younger birds sometimes complete their very first migration entirely alone, relying only on instincts passed down through generations.
__(5)__ , climate change is now disrupting the timing of migration, forcing birds to arrive at breeding grounds before enough food is available. Some species have __(6)__ adjusted their travel schedules, but many others are struggling to keep up with the rapidly changing environment. Conservationists warn that protecting resting sites along migration routes is now just as important as protecting the birds' original habitats.

title: A Lesson from My Failed Experiment
passage:
For the school science fair, I decided to build a small water filter using sand, charcoal, and gravel, believing it would easily win first prize, __(1)__ my confidence turned out to be completely misplaced. I had spent weeks imagining the compliments I would receive from judges and classmates alike. On the day of testing, the water that came out of my filter was cloudier than the water I had poured in. A few classmates standing nearby could not hide their surprised laughter at the unexpected result.
I had assumed that simply stacking materials in a bottle __(2)__ enough to purify water, without considering the correct order or thickness of each layer. My science teacher, __(3)__ feedback later helped me understand my mistake, pointed out that I had reversed the layers of sand and charcoal. She reminded me, gently but firmly, that even professional scientists often fail many times before succeeding.
Determined not to give up, I rebuilt the filter following proper instructions and tested it again three days later. This time, the water came out noticeably __(4)__ , and my teacher confirmed that the basic filtering process was finally working correctly. I felt an unexpected sense of relief, almost more meaningful than winning any prize could have felt.
My project did not win any prize at the fair. __(5)__ , I learned something far more valuable than a ribbon: real science requires patience, careful observation, and the willingness to learn from failure. That lesson has since changed the way I approach every difficult problem, both in science class and in daily life. __(6)__ , I now believe that a failed experiment can teach more than a perfect one ever could.

blanks.stem: I believed my filter would easily win first prize, __(1)__ my confidence turned out to be completely misplaced.

blanks.stem: My science teacher, __(3)__ feedback later helped me understand my mistake, pointed out that I had reversed the layers of sand and charcoal.
blanks.explanation: 表達「她的」回饋，需用所有格關係代名詞 whose。

blanks.stem: This time, the water came out noticeably __(4)__ , and my teacher confirmed that the filtering process was finally working correctly.
blanks.explanation: 重做濾水器後過濾效果改善，水變得「更清澈」（clearer），與先前失敗時的混濁形成對比。

blanks.stem: My project did not win any prize at the fair. __(5)__ , I learned something far more valuable than a ribbon.
blanks.explanation: 雖然沒得獎，但「儘管如此」仍學到更重要的事，Still 在此表達讓步後的轉折語氣。

blanks.stem: __(6)__ , I now believe that a failed experiment can teach more than a perfect one ever could.
blanks.explanation: 全文總結語氣，用 Overall（整體而言）做結論最恰當。

title: The Changing Face of Night Markets
passage:
For decades, night markets have served as the beating heart of social life in many Taiwanese towns, __(1)__ recent years have brought changes that older vendors could hardly have imagined. Cash registers __(2)__ replaced by mobile payment screens, and long lines are now often organized through phone apps instead of physical queues. Some vendors have even started livestreaming their cooking process to attract curious online viewers from across the island.
Many young entrepreneurs, __(3)__ family stalls have operated in the same market for generations, are redesigning traditional recipes to appeal to younger customers and foreign tourists. A classic stall that once sold only oyster omelets might now also offer a vegetarian version wrapped in colorful packaging for social media photos. Younger customers often photograph their food before eating, sharing it instantly with friends who live far away.
Some longtime vendors worry that these changes threaten the __(4)__ atmosphere that made night markets special in the first place. Others argue that adapting to new technology and tastes is simply necessary for survival in a competitive food industry. A few older vendors have even hired their grandchildren to help manage online orders and social media accounts.
__(5)__ , tourism officials note that international visitors are more digitally connected than ever, and reviews posted online can make or break a small stall's popularity almost overnight. A glowing review can send new customers rushing to a modest stall within hours of being posted.
__(6)__ these rapid changes, most vendors agree on one thing: the night market's true value has always come from the warmth of human connection, something no app or trend can fully replace.

blanks.stem: Night markets have served as the beating heart of social life in many Taiwanese towns, __(1)__ recent years have brought changes that older vendors could hardly have imagined.
blanks.explanation: 夜市長期是社交生活核心，但近年帶來難以想像的改變，前後語意轉折，用 but。

blanks.stem: Cash registers __(2)__ replaced by mobile payment screens.
blanks.explanation: 描述現在正在進行中的改變，須用現在進行式被動 are being replaced。

blanks.explanation: 表達「他們的」家族攤位，需用所有格關係代名詞 whose。

blanks.stem: Some longtime vendors worry that these changes threaten the __(4)__ atmosphere that made night markets special in the first place.
blanks.explanation: 老攤商擔心改變威脅到夜市原有的「道地」（authentic）氛圍，與後文語境呼應；其餘選項不合。

blanks.stem: __(5)__ , tourism officials note that international visitors are more digitally connected than ever.
blanks.explanation: 承接上段不同立場的討論，「同時」觀光官員也提出另一觀點，用 Meanwhile 銜接不同角度的資訊。

blanks.stem: __(6)__ these rapid changes, most vendors agree on one thing: the night market's true value has always come from human connection.
blanks.explanation: 「儘管」有這些快速的變化，攤商仍認同夜市的核心價值不變，用 Despite 表讓步。

title: The Silent Cost of Fast Fashion
passage:
Fast fashion has transformed the way people around the world buy and discard clothing, __(1)__ few consumers ever consider the hidden environmental and human costs behind a five-dollar T-shirt. Factories in developing countries now produce billions of garments each year, many of which are worn only a handful of times before being thrown away. Some clothing items are discarded after being worn just once, often ending up in landfills within months of purchase.
Textile production __(2)__ enormous amounts of water and energy, and the dyeing process alone contributes to a significant portion of global industrial water pollution. In some manufacturing regions, the color of a nearby river has become an unofficial indicator of the fashion industry's current trends. Rivers near manufacturing hubs, __(3)__ once-clear waters supported entire fishing communities, have in some cases turned toxic shades of blue, red, or black depending on the season's popular colors.
Beyond the environmental damage, labor conditions in many garment factories remain __(4)__ , with workers, most of them women, earning wages far below what is needed to live comfortably while working extremely long shifts. Reports have documented shifts lasting more than twelve hours, often in poorly ventilated factories with limited safety equipment. __(5)__ , a growing number of consumers are beginning to demand transparency, pushing some brands to publish information about their supply chains and factory conditions.
__(6)__ this small shift toward accountability, critics argue that true change will require consumers to buy fewer, higher-quality garments rather than simply choosing a slightly more ethical fast-fashion brand. Until spending habits change fundamentally, the industry's underlying problems are unlikely to disappear. For now, the true cost of a cheap T-shirt remains largely invisible to the customers who wear it.

blanks.stem: Fast fashion has transformed the way people buy and discard clothing, __(1)__ few consumers ever consider the hidden environmental and human costs behind a five-dollar T-shirt.
blanks.explanation: 快時尚改變了消費習慣，然而消費者鮮少思考背後代價，前後語意轉折，用 yet。

blanks.stem: Textile production __(2)__ enormous amounts of water and energy.
blanks.explanation: 描述紡織生產普遍且持續成立的事實，須用現在簡單式 requires。

blanks.stem: Rivers near manufacturing hubs, __(3)__ once-clear waters supported entire fishing communities, have in some cases turned toxic colors.
blanks.explanation: 表達「它們的」曾經清澈的水域，先行詞雖為 rivers（非人），仍用所有格關係代名詞 whose。

blanks.stem: Labor conditions in many garment factories remain __(4)__ , with workers earning wages far below what is needed to live comfortably.
blanks.explanation: 工人薪資過低、工時過長，勞動條件應為「嚴苛的」（harsh）；其餘選項與後述情況矛盾。

blanks.stem: __(5)__ , a growing number of consumers are beginning to demand transparency, pushing some brands to publish supply chain information.
blanks.explanation: 儘管前述問題嚴重，「儘管如此」越來越多消費者開始要求透明度，用 Nevertheless 表轉折。

blanks.stem: __(6)__ this small shift toward accountability, critics argue that true change will require consumers to buy fewer, higher-quality garments.
blanks.explanation: 「儘管」有這小小的問責轉變，評論者仍認為真正的改變需要更多，用 Despite 表讓步。

title: How Sleep Shapes the Teenage Brain
passage:
Many teenagers stay up late scrolling through their phones, __(1)__ growing research suggests that this habit may be quietly reshaping the way their brains develop. During adolescence, the brain undergoes significant restructuring, and sleep plays a crucial role in this delicate process. Researchers describe this period as a uniquely sensitive window during which lifelong habits and thinking patterns begin to take shape.
Scientists have found that during deep sleep, the brain __(2)__ memories from the day, strengthening connections that support learning while clearing away unnecessary information. Without enough of this deep sleep, students may find it noticeably harder to recall material they studied only the day before. Teenagers, __(3)__ natural sleep cycles shift later due to hormonal changes, often struggle to fall asleep before eleven at night, even when they intend to sleep earlier. As a result, many teenagers accumulate what scientists call a sleep debt that gradually builds up across the school week.
This mismatch between biological sleep needs and early school schedules can lead to chronic sleep deprivation, a condition linked to __(4)__ effects on mood, concentration, and decision-making. Some researchers have even connected insufficient sleep during teenage years to a higher risk of anxiety and difficulty regulating emotions later in life. Some studies have even suggested a connection between chronic teenage sleep loss and long-term changes in memory performance.
__(5)__ , several school districts have begun experimenting with later start times, and early results suggest modest improvements in student attendance and academic performance. __(6)__ , experts caution that changing school schedules alone cannot solve the problem without also addressing habits like excessive nighttime screen use. Parents, teachers, and students alike are being encouraged to treat sleep as seriously as diet and exercise.

blanks.stem: Many teenagers stay up late scrolling through their phones, __(1)__ growing research suggests that this habit may be quietly reshaping the way their brains develop.
blanks.explanation: 青少年熬夜滑手機，然而研究顯示此習慣可能影響大腦發展，前後語意轉折，用 yet。

blanks.stem: During deep sleep, the brain __(2)__ memories from the day, strengthening connections that support learning.
blanks.explanation: 描述睡眠時大腦運作的普遍科學事實，須用現在簡單式 processes。

blanks.explanation: 表達「他們的」自然睡眠週期，需用所有格關係代名詞 whose。

blanks.stem: Chronic sleep deprivation is a condition linked to __(4)__ effects on mood, concentration, and decision-making.
blanks.explanation: 慢性睡眠不足與情緒、專注力等負面影響有關，用 harmful（有害的）最符合語境；其餘選項語意矛盾或不合。

blanks.stem: __(5)__ , several school districts have begun experimenting with later start times.
blanks.explanation: 針對前述睡眠問題，「作為回應」一些學區開始試驗延後上課時間，用 In response 承接上文問題並提出對策。

blanks.stem: __(6)__ , experts caution that changing school schedules alone cannot solve the problem.
blanks.explanation: 雖然延後上課時間略有成效，「儘管如此」專家仍提醒不能只靠這點解決問題，用 Still 表轉折。

title: The Unspoken Rules of Apology
passage:
Saying "I'm sorry" seems simple enough, __(1)__ psychologists who study human relationships argue that a genuine apology is actually one of the most complex social skills a person can develop. A poorly delivered apology can sometimes damage a relationship more than staying silent altogether. This is because a clumsy attempt at an apology can unintentionally suggest that the harm caused was not fully understood.
Research on effective apologies __(2)__ that the most successful ones share several key elements: a clear acknowledgment of wrongdoing, an explanation that does not sound like an excuse, and a sincere expression of regret. Apologies, __(3)__ wording focuses too heavily on justification rather than responsibility, often fail to repair trust, even when the speaker's intentions are good. Listeners are often remarkably skilled at detecting when an apology is more about ending an awkward conversation than expressing real regret.
Cultural background also shapes how apologies are given and received; some cultures value indirect, __(4)__ expressions of regret, while others expect direct and explicit statements of fault. Misunderstanding these cultural differences can create friction even when both people genuinely wish to reconcile. International students, for instance, sometimes report feeling confused by apology customs that differ greatly from those in their home countries.
__(5)__ , the timing of an apology matters nearly as much as its content — an apology offered too quickly can feel insincere, while one offered too late may arrive after resentment has already taken root. __(6)__ , learning to apologize well requires not just good intentions but genuine self-awareness, patience, and the willingness to understand how one's actions affected another person. In the end, a truly effective apology says less about admitting fault and more about restoring trust between two people.

blanks.stem: Saying "I'm sorry" seems simple enough, __(1)__ psychologists argue that a genuine apology is actually one of the most complex social skills a person can develop.
blanks.explanation: 道歉看似簡單，然而心理學家認為這其實是複雜的社交技巧，前後語意轉折，用 yet。

blanks.stem: Research on effective apologies __(2)__ that the most successful ones share several key elements.
blanks.explanation: 描述研究上普遍且持續成立的發現，須用現在簡單式 shows。

blanks.explanation: 表達「其」措辭，先行詞雖為 apologies（非人），仍用所有格關係代名詞 whose。

blanks.stem: Some cultures value indirect, __(4)__ expressions of regret, while others expect direct and explicit statements of fault.
blanks.explanation: 與前文「間接」呼應，形容含蓄委婉的表達方式用 subtle 最貼切；其餘選項語意與間接表達直接衝突。
